license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgsnjwde
title: Tissue freezing in Cryostor solution + processing
author: Regina Hoo

steps_raw.instruction: Most tissue is collected in Hyperthermosol and kept on ice or at 4 °C .
Place tissue in petri dish, add enough ice cold RPMI/10% FBS to cover the tissue (depend on tissue size).
Mince tissue with 2 scapels, just like what we would do for enzymatic digestion.
Carefully transfer minced tissue into a 50ml falcon tube.
Wash and rinse the petri dish with plenty of RPMI/10% FBS. Transfer the RPMI/10%FBS and remaining tissue pieces into the falcon tube.
Resuspend the tissue in 1 ml of ice cod CS10 and transfer into a cryovial.
Rinse the falcon tube with 0.8 ml of extra cold CS10 and transfer into the cryovial.
Place cryovial in CoolCell and leave in -80 °C freezer overnight.
Remove cryovial from CoolCell next day, and store cryovial in designated box in -80 °C .
To process cryopreserved tissue, thaw cryovials in water bath at 37 °C .
Transfer thawed tissue into a falcon tube containing 20ml warm RPMI/10% FBS.
Centrifuge at 500 x g for 4 minutes.
Discard supernant. Proceed with preferred tissue digestion protocol
